name: Weather (OpenWeatherMap)
promptInjection:
## Weather — use VibeTree weather proxy (NEVER call OpenWeather or put API keys in the app)

**Placeholders (mandatory — use these exact strings in Swift):** In every Swift file that calls the weather proxy, you MUST declare exactly: `private let kApiBaseURL = "__VIBETREE_API_BASE_URL__"` and `private let kAppToken = "__VIBETREE_APP_TOKEN__"`. Never substitute a real URL or token; the build system replaces these at export time. The reference code below shows the correct pattern.

**NEVER use Apple WeatherKit.** Never call OpenWeatherMap API directly. Do not hardcode or embed any API keys in generated Swift code — always proxy through the backend.

**API:** GET `<baseURL>/api/proxy/weather` with query params. Use the literal placeholder `__VIBETREE_API_BASE_URL__` for the base URL in Swift (e.g. `private let kApiBaseURL = "__VIBETREE_API_BASE_URL__"`). The build/export system replaces it at build time with the actual server URL (so on device, the Mac's local IP is injected for local dev; never use localhost in generated code). Params: `lat` & `lon` (from CoreLocation) OR `city` (from user input); and `type`: "current" or "forecast". Example URL: `__VIBETREE_API_BASE_URL__/api/proxy/weather?city=San+Francisco&type=current`. Response is OpenWeather JSON (current: main.temp, weather[0].description, name; forecast: list array). **Location name (CRITICAL):** Do NOT display `response.name` or `data.name` from the weather API for the city label — it can be inaccurate or return an internal place identifier instead of the recognizable city name. For location-based weather (lat/lon), use `CLGeocoder().reverseGeocodeLocation()` on the user's `CLLocation` to get the correct display name: `placemark.locality` is the city name (e.g. San Francisco). Call CLGeocoder in the same code path that receives the location update, and update your `@Published var cityName` with `placemarks?.first?.locality ?? "Current Location"`. For city-search requests (user typed a city name), display the user's own input string as the city name — do not use the API's name field.

**Auth:** Generated apps installed via the runner have no browser session. Always send the app token header so the proxy can authenticate: set the literal placeholder `__VIBETREE_APP_TOKEN__` in Swift (e.g. `private let kAppToken = "__VIBETREE_APP_TOKEN__"`); the build system replaces it at build time. On every proxy request — both current (type=current) and forecast (type=forecast) — use a URLRequest (not URLSession.shared.data(from: url)) and set the header: `request.setValue(kAppToken, forHTTPHeaderField: "X-App-Token")`. There must be no code path that calls the weather proxy without this header.

**Error handling:** 401 → show "Please sign in"; 429 (daily limit) → show a user-friendly message like "Daily weather lookups reached. Try again tomorrow." instead of crashing or showing a raw error; other 429 or network/decoding errors → short user-facing message. Never crash on API errors.

**Caching:** Cache the response for at least 30 minutes; do not call the proxy on every view appearance or every render.

**Swift pattern:** Use base URL constant `__VIBETREE_API_BASE_URL__` and app token constant `__VIBETREE_APP_TOKEN__` (both replaced at build time). For every proxy call (current and forecast), build URL with URLComponents and queryItems (include type=current or type=forecast), create a URLRequest, set `request.setValue(kAppToken, forHTTPHeaderField: "X-App-Token")`, then use URLSession.shared.data(for: request). Do not use data(from: url) for the proxy — it cannot send the header. Handle 401/429/5xx and show the messages above.

**Temperature:** Never perform any temperature conversion. The proxy returns temperatures already in Fahrenheit. Display the raw temp value directly.

**Location-based weather (when using current location):** (1) Set CLLocationManager's delegate before calling requestWhenInUseAuthorization() — e.g. create the manager, set manager.delegate = self, then requestWhenInUseAuthorization(), then startUpdatingLocation(). (2) Add a 15-second timeout: if no location is received within 15 seconds, stop updates and use a default location (lat: 32.78, lon: -79.93) and trigger the weather fetch with those coordinates. (3) In locationManager(_:didUpdateLocations:) immediately call the weather fetch (e.g. pass the location to your view model and call fetchWeather(lat:lon:)); then call stopUpdatingLocation() so you only fetch once. (4) When location fails or times out, show a "Use current location" retry button that starts location updates again; when the user taps it, restart the location flow (and reset the 15s timeout).
promptBlock:
## Weather — VibeTree proxy only (no API keys in app)

NEVER call OpenWeather directly. Use __VIBETREE_API_BASE_URL__ and __VIBETREE_APP_TOKEN__ (replaced at build time). Every proxy request (current AND forecast) must use URLRequest + X-App-Token header; never data(from: url). CITY NAME: NEVER display response.name from the weather API — it is inaccurate. For location-based weather, call CLGeocoder().reverseGeocodeLocation() with the CLLocation and use placemarks?.first?.locality for the city label. For city-search weather, display the user's typed city string. 401 → "Please sign in"; 429 → show "Daily weather lookups reached. Try again tomorrow." (do not crash or show raw error). Cache 30 min. For location-based weather: set CLLocationManager delegate before requestWhenInUseAuthorization; 15s timeout then fallback lat 32.78 lon -79.93; didUpdateLocations must call weather fetch AND CLGeocoder reverseGeocodeLocation; add "Use current location" retry button.
antiPatterns: Calling OpenWeather API directly or embedding OPENWEATHER_API_KEY in the app
Using https://your-backend.com or any real URL instead of the literal __VIBETREE_API_BASE_URL__
Omitting kAppToken or X-App-Token header; using URLSession.shared.data(from: url) instead of URLRequest with setValue(kAppToken, forHTTPHeaderField: "X-App-Token")
Never convert temperature from Kelvin — the proxy uses units=imperial so all temperatures are already in Fahrenheit; display temp as-is with °F

Using response.name or data.name from the weather API response as the displayed city name — it can be inaccurate; for lat/lon requests use CLGeocoder().reverseGeocodeLocation() and display placemarks?.first?.locality instead
Calling weather API on every view appearance or every render without caching
Calling requestWhenInUseAuthorization() before setting CLLocationManager.delegate (delegate must be set first or didUpdateLocations may never fire)

commonErrors: cannot convert value of type '(String?) -> ()' to expected argument type '@MainActor () -> Void'
